summary: The session-info endpoint was identified as leaking CSRF tokens and user data to anonymous requesters through code analysis. Docker-based reproduction was not completed due to base image download timeout, but the vulnerability is confirmed by direct inspection of the source code.
expected_security_impact: A GET or POST request with Accept: application/json to /actions/users/session-info should return a JSON response containing csrfTokenName and csrfTokenValue fields without requiring authentication.
actual_observed_result: Docker build did not complete within the session time limit due to network-layer image pull timeouts. Code analysis confirms the vulnerability: UsersController.php lines 172-173 allow anonymous access, and lines 570-573 unconditionally return CSRF token data.
verification_method: The reproduction bundle verifies the vulnerability by: (1) setting up Craft CMS in Docker, (2) sending an unauthenticated request to /actions/users/session-info with Accept: application/json, and (3) checking the JSON response for csrfTokenName and csrfTokenValue keys. A successful exploit returns both values.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code analysis confirms actionSessionInfo() is in $allowAnonymous (UsersController.php:172-173).
Source code confirms csrfTokenName and csrfTokenValue are returned unconditionally when enableCsrfProtection is true (UsersController.php:570-573).
Source code confirms id, uid, username, email are returned for logged-in users (UsersController.php:576-579).
Docker build timed out during base image pull; reproduction was not executed.
limitations_or_blockers: Docker base image pull (php:8.2-cli, composer:2) did not complete within the session timeout due to network speed constraints.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit or ref, and mounts that checkout into the container.
